name: cace

metadata.name: cace
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
CACE

Circuit Automatic Characterization Engine

CACE is a framework for analog and mixed-signal circuits that enables automatic characterization under various conditions and with Monte Carlo and mismatch analysis. After all parameters have been run under the given conditions, CACE will generate a summary showing the circuit performance. > [!NOTE] > The latest documentation can be viewed online at [cace.readthedocs.io](https://cace.readthedocs.io/). ## Installation CACE currently supports two primary methods of installation for it and its dependencies. Please read the installation instruction in the documentation under ["Installation Overview"](https://cace.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting_started/index.html). ### Nix (Recommended) Works for macOS and Linux (x86-64 and aarch64) as well for Windows via WSL2. Recommended, as it is more integrated with your filesystem and overall has less upload and download deltas. See [Nix-based installation](https://cace.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting_started/common/nix_installation/index.html) in the docs for more info. ### Python-only Installation You'll need to bring your own compiled utilities, but otherwise, simply install CACE as follows: ```console python3 -m pip install --upgrade cace ``` ## Usage To invoke the CLI: ```console cace [datasheet] [output] [options] ``` For more information about the usage of CACE please have a look at ["Usage Guides"](https://cace.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage_guides/index.html) in the documentation. ## Examples There exist already numerous designs that use CACE. We have assembled a list of different designs that you can use as reference: [Example Designs](https://cace.readthedocs.io/en/latest/examples/index.html). ## License [The Apache License, version 2.0](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.txt).

functions.name: cace.common.spiceunits.numeric
documentation: Convert string to either integer or float, with priority on integer
If argument is not a string, just return the argument.

functions.name: cace.common.spiceunits.spice_unit_convert
documentation: Convert SI units into spice values
Define how to convert SI units to spice values
NOTE: spice_unit_unconvert can act on a tuple of (units, value) where
value is either a single value or a list of values.  spice_unit_convert
only acts on a tuple with a single value.  This is because the only large
vectors are produced by ngspice, and these values need unconverting back
into the units specified by the datasheet.  Values being converted to
ngspice units are from the datasheet and are only computed a few at a
time, so handling vectors is not particularly efficient.

functions.name: cace.common.spiceunits.spice_unit_unconvert
documentation: Convert spice values back into SI units
Define how to convert spice values back into SI units

functions.name: cace.common.misc.slugify
documentation:
---
param value: Input string
returns: |-
  The input string converted to lower case, with all characters
  except alphanumerics, underscores and hyphens removed, and spaces and dots converted into hyphens.

  Leading and trailing whitespace is stripped.
---

functions.name: cace.common.misc.protected
documentation:
A decorator to indicate protected methods.

It dynamically adds a statement to the effect in the docstring as well
as setting an attribute, `protected`, to `True`, but has no other effects.

```
param f

Method to mark as protected
```

functions.name: cace.common.misc.mkdirp
documentation:
Attempts to create a directory and all of its parents.

Does not fail if the directory already exists, however, it does fail
if it is unable to create any of the components and/or if the path
already exists as a file.

```
param path

A filesystem path for the directory
```

functions.name: cace.common.common.get_pdk
documentation: Get a value for the PDK, as environment variable "PDK".

functions.name: cace.common.common.get_pdk_root
documentation:
Get a value for PDK_ROOT, either from an environment variable, or
from several standard locations (open_pdks install and IIC-tools
install and volare install).
If found, set the environment variable PDK_ROOT.

functions.name: cace.common.common.get_magic_rcfile
documentation:
Get the path and filename of the magic startup script corresponding
to the PDK.

Returns a string containing the full path and filename of the startup
script (.magicrc file).

functions.name: cace.common.common.get_klayout_techfile
documentation:
Get the path and filename of the klayout tech file corresponding
to the PDK.

Returns a string containing the full path and filename of the tech
file (.lyt file).

functions.name: cace.common.common.get_klayout_layer_props
documentation:
Get the path and filename of the klayout layer properties corresponding
to the PDK.

Returns a string containing the full path and filename of the layer
properties (.lyp file).

functions.name: cace.common.common.get_netgen_setupfile
documentation:
Get the path and filename of the netgen setup script corresponding
to the PDK.

Returns a string containing the full path and filename of the setup
script (.tcl file).

functions.name: cace.common.common.set_xschem_paths
documentation:
Put together a set of Tcl commands that sets the search
path for xschem.

If tclstr is not None, then it is assumed to be a valid
Tcl command, and the rest of the Tcl command string is
appended to it, with independent commands separated by
semicolons.

Return the final Tcl command string.

Note that this is used only when regenerating the schematic
netlist. The testbenches are assumed to call the symbol as
a primitive, and rely on an include file to pull in the
netlist (either schematic or layout) from the appropriate
netlist directory.

functions.name: cace.common.common.xschem_generate_svg
documentation:
Generate an SVG drawing of a schematic or symbol using xschem

Return 0 if the drawing was generated, 1 if not.

functions.name: cace.common.common.magic_generate_svg
documentation:
Generate an SVG drawing of a layout using magic

Return 0 if the drawing was generated, 1 if not.

functions.name: cace.common.common.klayout_generate_png
documentation:
Generate a PNG drawing of a layout using klayout

Return 0 if the drawing was generated, 1 if not.

functions.name: cace.common.common.get_condition_names_used
documentation: Read a template file and record all of the variable names that will
be substituted, so it is clear which local and global conditions
need to be enumerated.  Vectors are reduced to just the vector name.
Returns a dictionary with keys corresponding to condition names;
the dictionary values are unused and just set to "True".

A generic ring (circular) buffer that automatically pops the element at the

modules.name: cace.common.safe_eval
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3
Implementation of "safe_eval", a function that implements an alternative
to python "eval()" that only executes arithmetic functions.
The implementation is taken from https://stackoverflow.com/
questions/15197673/using-pythons-eval-vs-ast-literal-eval

functions.name: cace.logging.logger.register_additional_handler
documentation:
Adds a new handler to the default CACE logger.

```
param handler

The new handler. Must be of type logging.Handler
or its subclasses.
```

functions.name: cace.logging.logger.deregister_additional_handler
documentation:
Removes a registered handler from the default CACE logger.

```
param handler

The handler. If not registered, the behavior
of this function is undefined.
```

functions.name: cace.logging.logger.set_log_level
documentation:
Sets the log level of the default CACE logger.

```
param lv

Either the name or number of the desired log level.
```

functions.name: cace.logging.logger.reset_log_level
documentation:
Sets the log level of the default CACE logger back to the
default log level.

functions.name: cace.logging.logger.get_log_level
documentation: Obtains the numeric log level of the CACE logger.

functions.name: cace.logging.logger.dbg

```
param msg

The message to log
```

documentation: Logs to the CACE logger with the log level VERBOSE.

```

functions.name: cace.logging.logger.subproc

documentation:

Kwargs are passed to <https://rich.readthedocs.io/en/stable/reference/console.html#rich.console.Console.rule>

```

A title string to enclose in the console rule

modules.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate
documentation:
These procedures are used by cace_gensim to check if netlists need
to be automatically regenerated, and to run schematic capture or
layout extraction as needed.
Copyright 2024 Efabless Corporation
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at
http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.printwarn
documentation: Print warnings output from a file run using the subprocess package

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.printall
documentation: Print all output from a file run using the subprocess package

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.check_layout_out_of_date
documentation:
Check if a netlist (spicepath) is out-of-date relative to the layouts
(layoutpath).  Need to read the netlist and check all of the subcells.

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.check_gds_out_of_date
documentation:
Check if the gds is out-of-date relative to the magic layout.
Need to generate the gds from the mag files.

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.check_schematic_out_of_date
documentation:
Check if a netlist (spicepath) is out-of-date relative to the schematics
(schempath).  Need to read the netlist and check all of the subcells.

This routine can also be used to determine if a testbench netlist is
up-to-date with respect to its schematic.

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.regenerate_netlist
documentation:
Regenerate the layout-extracted netlist if out-of-date or if forced.
If argument "pex" is True, then generate parasitic capacitances in
the output.

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.check_dependencies
documentation:
Check the datasheet for listed dependencies and make sure they exist.
If not, and the dependency entry lists a repository, then clone the
dependency.

Returns True if a dependency was cloned, and False if nothing needed
to be done.

To do:  For each dependency, find a CACE datasheet and read the path
information to find the path to schematics, so this can be used to
add the correct search path to the xschemrc file.  For now, it is
assumed that the path name is 'xschem'.

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.regenerate_schematic_netlist
documentation: Regenerate the schematic-captured netlist if out-of-date or if forced.

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.regenerate_testbench
documentation: Regenerate a testbench template (create SPICE from .sch)

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.regenerate_netlists
documentation: Regenerate all netlists as needed when out of date.

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.regenerate_gds
documentation: Regenerate gds as needed when out of date.

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.make_symbol_primitive
documentation:
Copy the schematic symbol to the testbench directory and remark its
type from 'schematic' to 'primitive', so that testbench netlists will
write an instance call but not the schematic itself.  That way, the
schematic can be brought in from an include file that can be set to
any one of schematic-captured or layout-extracted netlists.

functions.name: cace.common.cace_regenerate.regenerate_testbenches
documentation:
If pname is passed to regenerate_testbenches and is not None, then
only generate testbenches required by the specified parameter.

modules.name: cace.gui.tooltip
documentation:
Michael Lange <klappnase (at) freakmail (dot) de>
The ToolTip class provides a flexible tooltip widget for tkinter; it is based on IDLE's ToolTip
module which unfortunately seems to be broken (at least the version I saw).
INITIALIZATION OPTIONS:
anchor :        where the text should be positioned inside the widget, must be on of "n", "s", "e", "w", "nw" and so on;
                default is "center"
bd :            borderwidth of the widget; default is 1 (NOTE: don't use "borderwidth" here)
bg :            background color to use for the widget; default is "lightyellow" (NOTE: don't use "background")
delay :         time in ms that it takes for the widget to appear on the screen when the mouse pointer has
                entered the parent widget; default is 1500
fg :            foreground (i.e. text) color to use; default is "black" (NOTE: don't use "foreground")
follow_mouse :  if set to 1 the tooltip will follow the mouse pointer instead of being displayed
                outside of the parent widget; this may be useful if you want to use tooltips for
                large widgets like listboxes or canvases; default is 0
font :          font to use for the widget; default is system specific
justify :       how multiple lines of text will be aligned, must be "left", "right" or "center"; default is "left"
padx :          extra space added to the left and right within the widget; default is 4
pady :          extra space above and below the text; default is 2
relief :        one of "flat", "ridge", "groove", "raised", "sunken" or "solid"; default is "solid"
state :         must be "normal" or "disabled"; if set to "disabled" the tooltip will not appear; default is "normal"
text :          the text that is displayed inside the widget
textvariable :  if set to an instance of tkinter.StringVar() the variable's value will be used as text for the widget
width :         width of the widget; the default is 0, which means that "wraplength" will be used to limit the widgets width
wraplength :    limits the number of characters in each line; default is 150

WIDGET METHODS:
configure(**opts) : change one or more of the widget's options as described above; the changes will take effect the
                    next time the tooltip shows up; NOTE: follow_mouse cannot be changed after widget initialization

Other widget methods that might be useful if you want to subclass ToolTip:
enter() :           callback when the mouse pointer enters the parent widget
leave() :           called when the mouse pointer leaves the parent widget
motion() :          is called when the mouse pointer moves inside the parent widget if follow_mouse is set to 1 and the
                    tooltip has shown up to continually update the coordinates of the tooltip window
coords() :          calculates the screen coordinates of the tooltip window
create_contents() : creates the contents of the tooltip window (by default a tkinter.Label)
!/usr/bin/env python3

classes.name: cace.parameter.parameter_magic_area.ParameterMagicArea
documentation:
Determine bounds of the design geometry

"cond" should be one of "area", "width", or "height", and determines
what value is returned by the routine.

The routine reads the .mag or .gds file of the layout and returns
the width and height values in microns.  This is captured from
standard output and the requested result returned to the calling
routine.

classes.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.__init__
documentation: Initialize the object with a datasheet

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.load_datasheet
documentation:
Tries to load a datasheet from the given path.
YAML is preferred over text format.
Returns 0 on success and 1 on failure.
## datasheet functions ###

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.find_datasheet
documentation:
Check the search_dir directory and determine if there
is a .yaml or .txt file with the name of the directory, which
is assumed to have the same name as the project circuit.  Also
check subdirectories one level down.
Returns 0 on success and 1 on failure.

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.set_datasheet
documentation: Set a new datasheet

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.get_datasheet
documentation: Return the datasheet

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.set_default_runtime_options
documentation: Sane default values

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.set_default_paths
documentation: Sane default values

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.validate_runtime_options
documentation: Make sure the runtime options contain valid values

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.get_all_pnames
documentation: Return all parameter names
## simulation functions ####

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.find_parameter
documentation: Searches for the parameter with the name pname

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.queue_parameter
documentation: Queue a parameter for later execution

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.prune_running_threads
documentation: Remove threads that are either marked as done or have been canceled

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.num_parameters
documentation: Get the number of queued or running parameters

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.num_queued_parameters
documentation: Get the number of queued parameters

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.num_running_parameters
documentation: Get the number of running parameters

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.run_parameters_async
documentation: Start a worker thread to start parameter threads

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.run_parameters_thread
documentation: Called as a thread, starts the threads of queued parameters

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.join_parameters
documentation: Join all running parameter threads

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.run_parameters
documentation: Run parameters sequentially, note that simulations can still be parallelized

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.cancel_parameters
documentation: Cancel all parameters

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.cancel_queued_parameters
documentation: Cancel all queued parameters

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.cancel_running_parameters
documentation: Cancel all running parameters

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.cancel_parameter
documentation: Cancel a single parameter

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.cancel_queued_parameter
documentation: Cancel a single running parameter

methods.name: cace.parameter.parameter_manager.ParameterManager.cancel_running_parameter
documentation: Cancel a single running parameter

The ParameterManager manages the parameter queue
of physical and electrical parameters.
It also holds the datasheet and provides functions to
manipulate it.

functions.name: cace.cace_cli.cli
documentation:
Read a text file in CACE (ASCII) format 4.0, run
simulations and analysis on electrical and physical
parameters, as appropriate, and write out a modified
file with simulation and analysis results.

Read a template file and record all of the variable names that will

need to be enumerated.
Base class for all parameters.

modules.name: cace.cace_gui
documentation:
This is a Python tkinter script that handles local
project management.  Much of this involves the
running of ng-spice for characterization, allowing
the user to determine where a circuit is failing
characterization.
